title: ResMed Inc. (RMD) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of ResMed Inc. (RMD) compiled by Gemini. Economic Prospect Score: 81/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 14.5% growth rate reflects robust expected earnings growth, driven by massive market share gains following competitor missteps, the ongoing structural shift toward high-margin software subscriptions, and global under-penetration of sleep apnea diagnosis.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: Left as null per the analysis constraints regarding unverified or assumed numerical variables.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: Left as null per the analysis constraints regarding unverified or assumed numerical variables.

thesisTitle: The Razor and Blades Model of Sleep
thesisParagraphs: ResMed operates a highly lucrative "razor and blades" business model. While the initial sale of CPAP devices generates substantial revenue and initiates patient lock-in, the true driver of long-term Free Cash Flow is the recurring, high-margin sale of replacement masks and accessories. Once a patient adapts to a specific ResMed mask geometry, the friction of switching brands is high.

Furthermore, ResMed has brilliantly augmented this hardware ecosystem with its AirView software platform. By seamlessly integrating patient data for healthcare providers, ResMed creates a secondary layer of switching costs for the physician network. This dual-pronged lock-in enables sustained, double-digit growth and significant pricing power, protecting its robust margins.
verdictFooter: Based on actuals as of Mar 20, 2026. Data via S&P Global.

faq.question: Why is the computed Intrinsic Value set to null?
faq.answer: Because the terminal growth and discount rate assumptions were unable to be verified through strictly extracted metrics, they were left as null to prevent the injection of ungrounded estimates, consequently making the final DCF computation null.

faq.question: What is driving ResMed's rapid growth?
faq.answer: Growth is fueled by increasing global diagnosis rates of sleep apnea and a significant structural advantage gained following a major product recall by its primary competitor, allowing ResMed to capture outsized market share.

faq.question: What is the biggest risk to ResMed's valuation?
faq.answer: The primary risk is the long-term, widespread adoption of highly effective GLP-1 weight-loss drugs, which could potentially reduce the incidence of obesity-related obstructive sleep apnea, shrinking ResMed's total addressable market.

prospectVerdictDetail: ResMed Inc. primarily provides cloud-connectable medical devices for the treatment of sleep apnea, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and other respiratory conditions. The company operates in an attractive duopoly market structure. The essential nature of its devices, combined with the integration of its proprietary AirView software platform, generates a recurring revenue stream from high-margin replacement components like masks. This ecosystem creates substantial switching costs for healthcare providers and patients alike, underpinning a highly durable economic moat.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: ResMed commands strong competitive momentum. Its primary competitor faced significant product recalls, allowing ResMed to aggressively capture market share and establish itself as the undisputed market leader in cloud-connected sleep apnea devices.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: ResMed exhibits robust, double-digit revenue growth. Its growth significantly outpaces many broader medical device peers, driven by the vast, underpenetrated market of undiagnosed sleep apnea patients globally.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company has capitalized tremendously on the struggles of its main competitor, expanding its market share substantially. It continues to dominate the initial diagnosis and device setup phase, securing long-term customer pipelines.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: While it possesses strong market share, pricing power in the medical device sector is inherently constrained by insurance reimbursement rates and the purchasing power of large healthcare networks.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Product velocity is moderate due to strict FDA regulatory requirements. However, ResMed continues to iterate on software integration, remote patient monitoring, and miniaturizing its CPAP devices.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: ResMed's moat is built on high switching costs created by its integrated software ecosystem, significant regulatory barriers, and its formidable patent portfolio in respiratory technology.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Once a patient is set up with a ResMed CPAP machine, they are highly likely to purchase high-margin replacement masks and accessories from ResMed for the lifetime of the therapy. Furthermore, doctors using the AirView platform face friction in switching to a competitor's monitoring software.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: The AirView platform exhibits moderate data network effects. As millions of devices feed data into the system, ResMed can refine its algorithms, improve patient outcomes, and offer more value to healthcare providers, reinforcing its market position.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The company holds a robust intellectual property portfolio covering its mask designs and airflow technology. The complex FDA approval process serves as a massive regulatory barrier to entry for potential new disruptors.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: While manufacturing hardware requires capital, the software-as-a-service (SaaS) elements of its business and the recurring revenue from high-margin mask replacements create a highly capital-efficient financial profile.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment remains positive, though recently nuanced by the potential impact of GLP-1 weight loss drugs on the long-term total addressable market for sleep apnea devices.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts consistently revise estimates upward based on ResMed's execution in capturing market share and driving higher margins through software integration and recurring accessory sales.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The core narrative is overwhelmingly positive regarding execution and market dominance. However, headline risk exists surrounding the long-term effectiveness and adoption of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs potentially reducing the incidence of obesity-linked sleep apnea.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has executed brilliantly in navigating supply chain challenges and capitalizing on competitor missteps. Capital allocation is focused on high-return R&D, strategic software acquisitions (like MatrixCare), and returning capital to shareholders.

prospectKeyRisks: Widespread adoption of highly effective GLP-1 weight-loss drugs could reduce the total addressable market for obesity-related obstructive sleep apnea over the long term.
Changes to Medicare or private insurance reimbursement rates for CPAP machines and accessories could negatively impact revenue and margins.
The return of its primary competitor (Philips) to the market following its massive recall could intensify pricing competition and slow market share gains.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Increasing global awareness and diagnosis rates of sleep apnea, particularly in largely untapped emerging markets.
Continued growth and monetization of its high-margin SaaS platforms (AirView, MatrixCare) for out-of-hospital care management.
Further integration of artificial intelligence and machine learning into its monitoring platforms to improve patient adherence and outcomes.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored RMD at 81/100 and Opus at 79/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
